title: Delicate German Cabbage Dumplings
description: A cultured and velvety German dish featuring hispi sweetheart cabbage, served in a scallop shell with a lacquer glaze, perfect for a cozy dinner
instructions: In a large bowl, combine the flour, warm water, salt, and black pepper to make a dough.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 12 equal pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
Flatten each ball into a thin circle.
Place a tablespoon of shredded cabbage in the center of each circle.
Fold the dough over the cabbage to form a half moon shape and press the edges together to seal the dumpling.
Repeat with the remaining dough and cabbage.
Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and gently add the dumplings.
Cook the dumplings for 10 minutes or until they float to the surface.
While the dumplings are cooking, melt the unsalted butter in a small saucepan over medium heat.
Brush the lacquer glaze over the melted butter.
To serve, place a dumpling in each scallop shell and brush with the lacquer glaze mixture.
